questionHtml:
Several reactions of calcium carbonate with dilute hydrochloric acid are carried out at the same temperature.

CaCO3 (s) + 2HCl (aq) → CaCl2 (aq) + H2O (l) + CO2 (g)

Which reaction has the greatest rate?

meta.title: Several reactions of calcium carbonate with dilute hydrochloric acid are carried out at the same temperature. CaCO3 (s) + 2HCl (aq) → CaCl2 (aq) + H2O (l) + CO2 (g) Which reaction
breadcrumbLabels: 6.1 Collision theory and rates of reaction
